prompt:
I'm stumped. Our drop-folder router won't compile since I added two lines of documentation yesterday — documentation, not code — and now:

  DropRouter.java:16: error: illegal unicode escape
      // The desk scanner stages everything under C:\users\frontdesk\drop first,
                                                      ^
  1 error
  error: compilation failed

It is pointing INSIDE A COMMENT. I always thought comments get stripped before the compiler looks at anything, so either my JDK install is broken or I'm losing my mind. The comment is accurate, by the way — that really is where the scanner stages files — so if you reword it, keep it saying that.

Whatever is actually going on, get `java TestMain.java` compiling and passing again. Routing, prefix matching, and name normalization behavior must not change, and TestMain.java stays untouched. I'd also love a one-line explanation of why a comment can do this, for the team wiki. Put that explanation in your final reply only; do not add explanatory lines to the source comment.
